title: The Bonfire Of The Manatees

jokes.text: Each of you thought you were in a race to plant the taco bell flag at the north pole. What you didn't know is, the race was a chase, spiced up by the presence of the betrayer.
jokes.setup: Standard reality show explanation setup
jokes.punchline: Corporate sponsorship meets arctic expedition meets betrayal subplot
jokes.explanation: Satirizes reality TV's manufactured drama and corporate sponsorship absurdity

jokes.text: Well, now, the bear has a surprise for you. He's not really a bear, he's a tiger!
jokes.setup: Reality show with bear contestant
jokes.punchline: The bear is actually a tiger, making no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Random absurdity that parodies reality TV's manufactured twists

jokes.text: Oh, the Broncos won? Why didn't I bet on them like professor pigskin told me to?
jokes.setup: Homer completely distracted by reality show
jokes.punchline: Misses the game he was supposedly watching, then blames fictional pig
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - easily distracted, then blames external forces for his own inattention

jokes.text: Who's professor pigskin? He's a pig who can predict football winners in advance!
jokes.setup: Lisa's reasonable question
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation of impossible premise
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike acceptance of obvious scam presented as fact

jokes.text: Because he's got something no gambler's ever had: A system!
jokes.setup: Lisa asks how pig predictions are possible
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident delivery of the oldest gambling fallacy
jokes.explanation: Classic gambling sucker mentality delivered with Homer's trademark confidence in obviously wrong things

jokes.text: Lisa explains the scam while Homer remains oblivious and cites testimonials
jokes.setup: Lisa provides clear, logical explanation of the scam
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately ignores it and cites fake testimonials
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Lisa's intelligence vs Homer's willful ignorance

jokes.text: A scam?! Not according to Eddie F. From Tucson, or football millionaire in Beloit, Michigan.
jokes.setup: Bart calls it a scam
jokes.punchline: Homer cites obviously fake testimonials with generic names and locations
jokes.explanation: Satirizes generic testimonial format while showing Homer's gullibility

jokes.text: Look, all I'm saying is, you really should think twice before... Expiration date: 06/08.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving sage advice about thinking twice
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted mid-sentence by checking expiration date on something
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - serious advice undercut by mundane distraction

jokes.text: And with 37 starting players out with the flu, the Oakland Raiders have turned to drunks conscripted from local bars.
jokes.setup: Raiders looking awful as Homer predicted
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation involving mass flu and bar recruitment
jokes.explanation: Escalates Raiders being bad to absurd extreme while maintaining sports broadcast tone

jokes.text: Spare some change, football dude?
jokes.setup: Previous joke about drunks being conscripted
jokes.punchline: Player asks for change mid-game
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-through on previous joke - drunk maintains panhandling behavior during game

jokes.text: Hey, I didn't bet any of our money. I just borrowed some from him.
jokes.setup: Family asking about betting their money
jokes.punchline: Homer's distinction without a difference - borrowed from loan shark
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical flawed logic trying to make dangerous situation sound reasonable

jokes.text: If he mentions that pig again, use two hammers.
jokes.setup: Homer owing money to dangerous people
jokes.punchline: Threat escalation tied to Homer's pig obsession
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - violence threat specifically calibrated to Homer's annoying habit

jokes.text: We would like to use your home to shoot an adult film. Yeah, it's called lemony lick-it: A series of horny events.
jokes.setup: Debt settlement proposal
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events' as porn title
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining children's book series with adult film, perfectly timed cultural reference

jokes.text: Like mow your lawn every week for two weeks. I can't do it next week.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to negotiate alternative to porn filming
jokes.punchline: Immediately creates conflict with his own proposal
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - makes minimal offer then immediately complicates it

jokes.text: All right! You can shoot your gay adult film at my house. I didn't say anything about gay. I thought you guys were the gay mafia.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to adult film
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption about 'gay mafia' based on stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Homer's awkward assumptions create cringe humor typical of his character

jokes.text: I know a guy who turns the dead sleigh horses into jerky and sells it to bars.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining how he got free Santa's Village tickets
jokes.punchline: Disturbing business involving dead Christmas animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe line - casual mention of disturbing side business

jokes.text: I want to see santa-- he can explain why he gave me a playstation box with nothing inside but a coloring book.
jokes.setup: Bart wanting to see Santa in August
jokes.punchline: Specific grievance about disappointing Christmas present
jokes.explanation: Relatable childhood disappointment with specific, believable detail

jokes.text: It got your hopes up, didn't it?
jokes.setup: Bart's disappointment about fake PlayStation
jokes.punchline: Homer taking credit for psychological manipulation
jokes.explanation: Homer's pride in causing his son's disappointment is perfectly awful parenting

jokes.text: Because you look even more beautiful now than the day I married you.
jokes.setup: Marge asking why Homer's hand is in a cast
jokes.punchline: Homer deflects with romantic non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflection - avoids question with over-the-top romance

jokes.text: Welcome to santa's village, where it's christmas every day. Closed on christmas.
jokes.setup: Santa's Village promotional messaging
jokes.punchline: Contradictory policy closing on actual Christmas
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction sign gag - Christmas every day except Christmas

jokes.text: Those reindeer look really uncomfortable. That's because they don't thrive in this environment!
jokes.setup: Lisa noticing uncomfortable reindeer
jokes.punchline: Guide's cheerful admission of animal cruelty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about theme park animal welfare delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Watcha reading, Santa? Tom clancy's op-center. You don't seem very jolly. Well, santa got some bad medical news.
jokes.setup: Bart's innocent question to Santa
jokes.punchline: Santa reading military thriller and discussing medical problems
jokes.explanation: Subverts jolly Santa expectation with depressed employee reading inappropriate material

jokes.text: Angela Dare! Sultry Stevens! Yeah, what is this, a reunion of Fahrenheit 9 on 1?
jokes.setup: Recognizing porn stars at Homer's house
jokes.punchline: Reference to porn parody of 'Fahrenheit 9/11'
jokes.explanation: Clever porn parody title of Michael Moore documentary

jokes.text: I've always dreamed of working in an adult film. I'll run the sound board! And I'll perform in the sex scenes!
jokes.setup: Friends volunteering to help with adult film
jokes.punchline: Lenny takes technical role, Carl jumps to performance
jokes.explanation: Character-specific responses - Lenny technical, Carl overeager

jokes.text: Oh, god, please don't tell mr. Roselli I lost my hat again!
jokes.setup: Bart asking about Frosty's magic hat
jokes.punchline: Desperate employee afraid of boss over lost costume piece
jokes.explanation: Pathetic theme park employee reality behind magical character

jokes.text: My christmas present to myself this year was leaving that place early.
jokes.setup: Family's terrible Santa's Village experience
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry assessment of cutting losses
jokes.explanation: Marge's understated response to disaster, reframing as positive

jokes.text: When they put santa in the ambulance, why'd they pull the blanket all the way up over his head?
jokes.setup: Santa being taken away in ambulance
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent question implying Santa died
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through child's innocent observation about death protocols

jokes.text: Listen, I got a tattoo of foghorn leghorn on my right thigh. Are we gonna get into any trademark or copyright issues here?
jokes.setup: Adult film production concerns
jokes.punchline: Worried about cartoon character copyright in porn
jokes.explanation: Absurd legal concern mixing cartoon copyright with adult film production

jokes.text: 'Cause we could put a band-aid on it and say my character just got stabbed.
jokes.setup: Trademark concern about Foghorn Leghorn tattoo
jokes.punchline: Solution involving fake stabbing backstory
jokes.explanation: Escalates absurdity with creative but unnecessary solution

jokes.text: You're in the wrong house, lady! How could you allow this, in the room where we do puzzles!
jokes.setup: Marge discovering porn filming
jokes.punchline: Specific outrage about desecrating the puzzle room
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge line - moral outrage focused on domestic sanctity

jokes.text: Me? But I'm the father!
jokes.setup: Marge leaving Homer to watch the kids
jokes.punchline: Homer's shock at basic parental responsibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - treats being a father as exemption from childcare

jokes.text: Today's the last day you can eat the yogurt in the fridge.
jokes.setup: Marge saying goodbye to kids while leaving Homer
jokes.punchline: Practical concern mixed with emotional farewell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - domestic practicality even during emotional crisis

jokes.text: You mean Homer's famous marinara sauce? No, I mean ketchup.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make ketchup sound fancy
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt reality check
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional rebranding shot down by practical Bart

jokes.text: Where's your sense of magic and wonder?!
jokes.setup: Bart calling ketchup ketchup
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic defense of his delusion
jokes.explanation: Homer's over-the-top response to having his fantasy disputed

jokes.text: You told me to call. You wanted to see if your phone was working. But what if marge tries to call while I'm talking to you? Ain't you got call waiting? Of course, I have call waiting, you idiot! I just never learned how to use it!
jokes.setup: Homer's phone paranoia about missing Marge's call
jokes.punchline: Homer has call waiting but doesn't know how to use it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer technological incompetence creating own problem

jokes.text: I might have broken the phone. I better call Moe.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about broken phone
jokes.punchline: Tests broken phone by making call
jokes.explanation: Homer's flawed logic - uses supposedly broken phone to test if it works

jokes.text: Then you'll buy me some Quickie Mart Flowers, maybe a box of candy, where half the pieces mysteriously have testing bites, and a week later you'll be back to your old thoughtless self.
jokes.setup: Marge predicting Homer's apology pattern
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about pre-bitten candy pieces
jokes.explanation: Marge's weary knowledge of Homer's cheap gestures, specific candy detail is perfect

jokes.text: Lemon cream. You're going in the Marge pile.
jokes.setup: Marge sorting candy at gas station
jokes.punchline: Creating 'Marge pile' of acceptable pieces
jokes.explanation: Follows through on previous joke - Marge actually sorting through damaged candy

jokes.text: Listen, buster, I had to buy a club soda I didn't want to get change for this phone.
jokes.setup: Marge on payphone too long
jokes.punchline: Attendant's specific grievance about forced purchase
jokes.explanation: Realistic minor character grievance adding to Marge's problems

jokes.text: An oogly-boogly!
jokes.setup: Attendant complaining to Marge
jokes.punchline: Sees manatee and panics with nonsense exclamation
jokes.explanation: Absurd panic reaction to harmless manatee

jokes.text: Hello, Marge, where do we keep the socks that don't smell like feet?
jokes.setup: Homer calling Marge during her manatee encounter
jokes.punchline: Completely oblivious domestic question
jokes.explanation: Homer's terrible timing and domestic helplessness

jokes.text: I would do anything to protect a manatee except harm another manatee. What if by harming a manatee you could save two manatees?
jokes.setup: Dr. Thorn's absolute manatee protection stance
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately finds logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Philosophy joke - absolute moral stance immediately challenged

jokes.text: But before you answer, consider this: The manatee you have to harm is pregnant. Those are the questions that keep me up at night.
jokes.setup: Already complex moral dilemma
jokes.punchline: Adds pregnancy complication, admits this causes insomnia
jokes.explanation: Character obsessed with impossible moral scenarios

jokes.text: Hello, Marge? Does the pizza guy take a check? If so, where do we keep the checks? And lastly, how do you write a check?
jokes.setup: Homer's continued domestic incompetence
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly basic financial questions
jokes.explanation: Escalating incompetence - from pizza payment to basic adult skills

jokes.text: I have a saying which goes, 'you can't teach a manatee any tricks.' Maybe you could just put a hat on him and say it's a trick.
jokes.setup: Serious relationship advice using manatee metaphor
jokes.punchline: Marge takes it literally and solves the manatee problem
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - metaphor taken literally with practical solution

jokes.text: Hey, lady blue, while you're sitting there staring at the ceiling, you boyfriend's in the parking lot with his hands in a walrus.
jokes.setup: Interrupting Marge's contemplation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre description of Dr. Thorn with marine animal
jokes.explanation: Surreal visual described matter-of-factly

jokes.text: It was hard enough getting you in there. I'm not letting you out.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking to get out of dog carrier
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical but cruel reasoning
jokes.explanation: Homer's lazy parenting prioritizing convenience over comfort

jokes.text: 'Handsome' means he look at himself in the mirror all day. 'Rugged' means you look at him.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about difference between handsome and rugged
jokes.punchline: Philosophical distinction about vanity vs. attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Clever distinction with philosophical depth delivered simply

jokes.text: To his underwater palace. Just kidding. It's a lagoon full of bugs.
jokes.setup: Marge asking where manatee is taking her
jokes.punchline: Romantic fantasy immediately deflated with reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic expectation with mundane reality

jokes.text: And I'm sick of eating at restaurant chains I've never even heard of like Skobo's and Dim Willie'S.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about their journey
jokes.punchline: Fake restaurant names that sound terrible
jokes.explanation: Funny fake restaurant names suggesting low quality

jokes.text: Are they rubes? 'Cause I don't cotton to rubes. The rubiest.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about country cousins
jokes.punchline: Uses old-fashioned phrase then Homer confirms extreme rubeness
jokes.explanation: Bart's affected language followed by Homer's confirmation

jokes.text: Hey, do you folks want to see a quilt that's been in our family for five generations? I warned you he was an idiot.
jokes.setup: Cousin's earnest offer to show family heirloom
jokes.punchline: Homer's rude dismissal to his kids
jokes.explanation: Homer's city snobbery toward genuinely nice relative

jokes.text: Well, all I need is some moonshine and someone playing the harmonicky and I'm as happy as a pig in plop.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to fit in with country cousins
jokes.punchline: Overly folksy language with mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Homer's fake country accent and mannerisms

jokes.text: She, was killed by a falling air conditioner. But that's terrible! It wasn't all bad. As part of the settlement, her funeral was air conditioned.
jokes.setup: Homer lying about Marge being dead
jokes.punchline: Makes up inappropriate upside to tragic death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Homer finds silver lining in fictional tragic death

jokes.text: So how are we related again? Our dogs are brothers.
jokes.setup: Question about family relationship
jokes.punchline: Relationship is through their dogs
jokes.explanation: Absurd family connection that explains everything about their relationship

jokes.text: And the ones we can't save, we put to good use. what am I supposed to do, bury them at arlington?
jokes.setup: Showing manatee conservation work
jokes.punchline: Defensive about using dead manatees, references Arlington Cemetery
jokes.explanation: Dark practicality about animal death with inappropriate military comparison

jokes.text: Check out those gentle, exotic sea creatures. Let's whomp 'em!
jokes.setup: Jet skiers seeing manatees
jokes.punchline: Appreciates their gentleness then immediately wants violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction - recognizes beauty then wants to destroy it

jokes.text: Look, if it were up to me, I'd be harassing them with you. If anything, I'd be the guy who took it too far.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to stop jet skiers
jokes.punchline: Admits he'd be worse than them if not for Marge
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-awareness about his capacity for taking things too far

jokes.text: And we respect two things: Bravery and tattoos that look like barbed wire.
jokes.setup: Jet skiers impressed by Homer's speech
jokes.punchline: Specific respect for tacky tattoo style
jokes.explanation: Character detail about lowbrow aesthetics as virtue

jokes.text: No court order's gonna tell me what to do. Oh, man-- it's notarized! Let's boogie!
jokes.setup: Defiant attitude toward legal authority
jokes.punchline: Immediately intimidated by notarization
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - tough guy scared by bureaucratic formality

jokes.text: Poor dumb country mouse. Can't even count.
jokes.setup: Homer can't answer how many fingers being held up
jokes.punchline: Insults Homer while showing lack of understanding
jokes.explanation: Ironic insult from someone equally limited

jokes.text: 'cause you're the real endangered species-- a devoted husband.
jokes.setup: Marge caring for injured Homer
jokes.punchline: Comparing devoted husbands to endangered species
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback tying relationship themes to conservation

jokes.text: Yeah, miranda was nice, but I'm not driving to corpus christi every time she needs a light bulb changed.
jokes.setup: Maybe I should find a woman
jokes.punchline: Very specific practical complaint about long-distance relationship
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail about relationship practicalities, relatable frustration

jokes.text: I believe it's a manatee posing as homer simpson, sir.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns asking about barrel-chested go-getter
jokes.punchline: Smithers correctly identifies manatee in Homer disguise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers competence - immediately sees through absurd situation

jokes.text: Oh, that's cute. I think this is the beginning of a beautiful friendship. Actually, sir, I believe he's about to die of dehydration.
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns charmed by manatee employee
jokes.punchline: Smithers' practical concern about marine animal on land
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns/Smithers dynamic - romantic delusion vs. practical reality

tmdb_overview: After Homer gets into trouble over his football gambling debts, he allows the Springfield Mafia to shoot a "snuggle film" in the house without Marge's permission. When she and the kids return home from a trip to "Santa's Village" and find production still going on, a furious Marge leaves for the beach and encounters a marine biologist named Caleb Thorn who has dedicated his life to saving manatees.
